statement: For a healthy resting adult, core body temperature is reasonably modeled as tightly centered near 37.0 °C, with ordinary circadian and measurement variation captured by a Normal prior with standard deviation about 0.3 °C.
source: Guyton and Hall Textbook of Medical Physiology

statement: For a healthy adult at rest, heart rate is commonly near 70 beats per minute, with typical inter-individual variation represented by a Normal prior with a standard deviation of about 10 beats per minute.
source: Bates' Guide to Physical Examination and History Taking

statement: For a resting adult without known cardiovascular disease, systolic blood pressure can be anchored near 120 mmHg, with age, body size, and measurement-setting variation approximated by a Normal prior with standard deviation about 12 mmHg.

For a healthy adult at sea level breathing room air, resting pulse oxygen saturation is usually high, around 97.5%; a Beta prior with alpha 236.7 and beta 6.1 represents a mean near 0.975 and standard deviation near 0.01 on the proportion scale.
source: World Health Organization Pulse Oximetry Training Manual

statement: For a healthy adult at rest, tidal volume is commonly approximated as 500 mL per breath, with ordinary body-size variation represented by a Normal prior with standard deviation about 100 mL.

For healthy adults, total blood volume is often approximated near 70 mL per kg of body mass, with sex and body-composition differences captured by a Normal prior with standard deviation about 8 mL/kg.
source: Guyton and Hall Textbook of Medical Physiology

statement: For a metabolically healthy adult after an overnight fast, fasting plasma glucose is commonly near 90 mg/dL; a Normal prior with standard deviation 10 mg/dL keeps most mass within the usual normoglycemic range.
source: American Diabetes Association Standards of Care in Diabetes

statement: For healthy adults, serum sodium concentration is tightly regulated around 140 mmol/L; a Normal prior with standard deviation 2.5 mmol/L places most probability within the conventional 135 to 145 mmol/L reference interval.
source: Tietz Textbook of Clinical Chemistry and Molecular Diagnostics

statement: For an unconditioned mixed adult population, basal metabolic rate is positive and right-skewed; a LogNormal prior with natural-log mu 7.38 and sigma 0.25 gives a median near 1600 kcal/day and allows moderate variation due to sex, age, and body size.

For a healthy adult at rest, blood lactate concentration is typically close to 1.0 mmol/L and strictly positive; using the Gamma rate parameterization, alpha 11.1 and beta 11.1 imply a mean near 1.0 mmol/L and standard deviation near 0.3 mmol/L.
source: McArdle, Katch, and Katch, Exercise Physiology: Nutrition, Energy, and Human Performance

statement: For healthy community-dwelling adults walking at a comfortable self-selected pace, speed is commonly around 1.3 to 1.4 m/s; a Normal prior centered at 1.34 m/s with standard deviation 0.20 m/s captures typical adult gait variability.
source: Perry and Burnfield, Gait Analysis: Normal and Pathological Function
